bead.title: agent/land: release withMainGitLock around network I/O (push, fetch, merge)
bead.description:
PROBLEM
  Land() in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go invokes git fetch
  origin, git push --ff-only, and a push-race auto-recovery loop as part of
  the core drain lifecycle. This couples Land() to remote I/O, holding
  withMainGitLock for 30-120s on remote round-trips, which produced the
  observed contention failures (ddx-08e90869, ddx-15a51f8c). Per the user's
  architectural position and the sister doc-only bead amending FEAT-010,
  Land() must perform ONLY the local worktree-merge into local main; origin
  sync (fetch) and push are separate operations, never invoked from Land()
  or the drain loop.

  This bead removes all network I/O from Land() and from the LandingGitOps
  interface. Origin-sync and push become operator responsibilities (raw
  git commands today; a future ddx sync helper is downstream of multi-node
  work and explicitly out of scope per operator direction).

ROOT CAUSE
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:27-29 file-header comment
    declares "If an origin remote exists — push the new target tip" as
    step 5 of the canonical Land flow.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1036-1043 Land() wraps the
    entire landLocked() call under withMainGitLock, including push.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1146-1216 landLocked merge
    path issues git fetch origin (FetchBranch), then git push (PushFFOnly),
    then on push race calls landPushAutoRecover.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1479 landPush triggers
    landPushAutoRecover on non-FF rejection.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:1538-1580 landPushAutoRecover
    runs fetch + temp-worktree merge + update-ref + push, all inside the
    same lock.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go:239-297 LandingGitOps
    interface includes FetchBranch, PushFFOnly, and FetchOriginAncestryCheck
    methods that exist solely to support the in-Land network I/O path.
  - LandResult fields PushFailed, PushConflict, PushRecovered, and the
    fetchOriginError type all exist to communicate network-I/O outcomes
    that Land() should no longer be responsible for.

PROPOSED FIX
  - Strip Land() to local-only operations:
      a. fetch-origin call (FetchBranch) at the start of landLocked:
         REMOVE.
      b. FetchOriginAncestryCheck before merge: REMOVE (the merge proceeds
         on local main as-is; if origin diverged, that is discovered when
         the operator pushes, by raw git push rejecting non-FF, which is
         the operator's problem).
      c. PushFFOnly at end of landLocked: REMOVE.
      d. landPushAutoRecover function: REMOVE entirely.
  - Update LandingGitOps interface:
      a. Remove FetchBranch method.
      b. Remove PushFFOnly method.
      c. Remove FetchOriginAncestryCheck method.
      d. Keep merge / update-ref / worktree / commit methods.
  - Update LandResult:
      a. Remove PushFailed, PushConflict, PushRecovered fields.
      b. Remove fetchOriginError type.
      c. Keep merge / commit / preserve outcomes.
  - Update the file-header comment at lines 27-29 to reflect the
    network-free flow.
  - Update Land() critical section: withMainGitLock now wraps only the
    local-index slice (merge-or-update-ref + evidence commit + worktree
    sync). Sub-second hold expected.
  - Existing tests for push-race recovery (and any LandResult.PushFailed
    assertions) MUST be removed or rewritten as no-network tests.
  - Add a regression test TestLand_IsNetworkFree in
    cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go that wires a
    LandingGitOps with no Fetch/Push methods (proving they are no longer
    called) and asserts Land() succeeds.

  Operator-side note (NOT in scope of this bead): users push with raw
  `git push origin main` after a drain session. A future ddx sync helper
  is downstream of multi-node coordination work and is explicitly
  deferred.

NON-SCOPE
  - Designing/building a ddx sync helper (deferred until multi-node).
  - Federation work (FEAT-026 / SD-020 build-out) will reintroduce
    coordinated network I/O with its own design; this bead is single-node
    only.
  - Updating prompt templates / eval data / closed beads that reference
    push_failed semantics (downstream impact; file separate beads if any
    surface friction).
  - Changing TD-031 bead state machine.
  - Reducing the bead.Store WithLock primitive (different layer).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Depends on doc-only spec amendment bead ddx-3d5197d8 landing first.
  - Sister chaos bead ddx-c019e48a (now scoped to local-only stale-lock
    recovery) depends on this implementation landing.
  - Sister chaos bead ddx-eab41b94 (push-race scenarios) is now obsolete
    and should be cancelled.
bead.acceptance:
1. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go Land() invokes no git fetch and no git push; LandingGitOps interface no longer declares FetchBranch, PushFFOnly, or FetchOriginAncestryCheck methods.
2. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land.go file-header comment (formerly lines 27-29) describes a network-free flow with no push step.
3. landPushAutoRecover function and any LandResult fields naming push outcomes (PushFailed, PushConflict, PushRecovered) are removed; fetchOriginError type is removed.
4. withMainGitLock in Land() wraps only local-index operations (merge or update-ref + evidence commit + worktree sync); a unit test asserts the lock is released within 1s under a fake LandingGitOps with simulated 5s of post-Land work in the caller (proving the lock does not extend beyond Land() return).
5. TestLand_IsNetworkFree in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_land_test.go wires a fake LandingGitOps with no fetch/push methods (compile-time proof) and asserts Land() succeeds end-to-end.
6. Existing tests that asserted push-race recovery (e.g. TestLandPushAutoRecover_*) are removed or rewritten; no test references LandResult.PushFailed.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
